mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 7.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 7.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of rising consumer credit and elevated housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving earnings growth and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined z-score of RSAFS and HOUST shows no predictive Rank IC > 0.05 for 20-day SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, a contraction in industrial production relative to demand signals supply-side bottlenecks that sustain inflation and pressure corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when INDPRO growth falls below GDPC1 growth during high-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic resilience that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes remains below 0.02 across all 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI index exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when CPIAUCSL monthly changes exceed the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between rising consumer credit and stagnant industrial production indicates demand-pull inflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins, producing negative equity returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The spread between RSAFS growth and INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sudden spike in the unemployment rate combined with falling federal funds rates indicates a policy pivot to avert recession, triggering a sharp positive equity repricing over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month AND FEDFUNDS declines
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not significantly outperform baseline when UNRATE spikes and FEDFUNDS cuts occur simultaneously (t-test p > 0.05).
